labels.1103: The feature fires on phrases that introduce or describe a subject, product, or event about to be elaborated.
labels.1552: URLs or web links being introduced or referenced, typically preceded by transitional phrases like "at," "to," or "go to."
labels.1629: Capitalized words or phrases at the start of titles, product names, or headlines.
labels.1656: Press release or news article dateline and source attribution markers.
labels.2113: Four-digit years at the end of dates or temporal references.
labels.2587: Capitalized phrases or titles introducing new topics or sections.
labels.2601: Capitalized multi-word phrases or titles that introduce or describe events, products, or concepts.
labels.3611: Years in the format "20XX" where the feature activates on the digits after "20".
labels.4173: Obituary text describing who preceded someone in death or who survives them.
labels.4677: Capitalized proper nouns or brand names at the start of phrases or titles.
labels.5165: Capitalized or emphasized words/phrases at the start of sentences or titles.
labels.5252: Capitalized words or proper nouns that begin sentences or sections.
labels.5678: Capitalized proper nouns or title case phrases marking beginnings of named entities, brands, or sentence starts.
labels.6257: Phone number and contact information digits in various formatting patterns.
labels.7187: Phrases that begin articles, titles, or sentences in news headlines and web content.
labels.7212: Words appearing at the beginning of sentences or text fragments, often capitalized proper nouns or sentence-initial pronouns.
labels.7297: Postal and zip codes from addresses in various countries and regions.
labels.8748: Stock ticker symbols and exchange codes in parentheses following company names.
labels.9334: Words or phrases being defined or explained through etymology, translation, or meaning clarification.
labels.9765: Five-digit US postal codes or ZIP codes embedded within longer alphanumeric sequences.
labels.9855: Location names and cities followed by news wire service abbreviations or date/source markers in press release headers.
labels.10269: Titles or headings of articles, sections, or product categories that introduce topics.
labels.10276: The feature fires on the boundary between content sections or topic transitions in web text and documents.
labels.10798: Tokens that are parts of compound words or phrases being split across word boundaries, particularly "SINGINGTELEGRAM" and "AUCKLAND" being broken mid-word.
labels.10811: Descriptions of content organization, ordering, or categorization methods.
labels.11798: Page or statute citation numbers appearing between hyphens or commas in legal and academic references.
labels.12349: Copyright and reproduction restriction clauses prohibiting unauthorized reuse of content.
labels.12410: Phrases that appear as clickable titles or headings in web content, often followed by descriptive text or metadata.
labels.12882: Timestamps with hour and minute values in 24-hour or 12-hour time format.
labels.13417: Repeated or emphasized phrases that appear at the beginning of content sections or headers.
labels.13436: Timestamps and date-time metadata with timezone offsets or time zone indicators.
labels.13834: Words that appear at the beginning of sentences or clauses, often capitalized and marking new ideas or topics.
labels.14355: Ampersands and special characters appearing at or near token boundaries in product names, titles, or organizational entities.
labels.15373: Email addresses or domain names in contact information contexts.
labels.15422: The feature activates at boundaries between distinct topics or content sections marked by line breaks or transitions.
labels.15465: Capitalized words or phrases that begin sentences or titles, often signaling new information or headings.
labels.15468: Phone number digit sequences within formatted telephone numbers.
labels.15491: Phrases introducing news topics or specific entities at the start of headlines.
labels.15492: Website URLs or domain names appearing in text, typically at sentence or phrase boundaries.
labels.15921: Advertisements or advertising-related terms appearing in contexts about serving ads on websites or adware displaying ads.
labels.16460: Placeholder email addresses or email contact information in the format firstname.lastname@example.com or similar dummy email patterns.
labels.16932: Incomplete or fragmented phrases at sentence boundaries where text appears corrupted or poorly tokenized.
labels.17535: Mentions of discount department store brands in comparative business contexts.
labels.17925: Possessive or descriptive phrases preceding nouns or titles that introduce names, products, or topics.
labels.18019: The feature activates on capitalized words or phrases that begin sentences or major text sections.
labels.18041: Phrases describing geographic locations, entities, or descriptive lists separated by commas within a bounded span.
